Refs #11377 (implementation/planning ticket; merge-blocked until this ADR Accepted)

Graduates Discussion #11374 (RESOLVED 2026-05-14T21:33:39Z; 3× APPROVED Signal Ledger across Cycles 1-5)

## Summary

Lands `learn/agentos/decisions/0006-adrs-as-graph-queryable-entities.md` as the authority artifact for ADR-as-graph-entity substrate. Operator-directed quick-win post-graduation:

> *"so, we have one graduated sandbox. creating e.g. the new ADR => quick win, logical next step."*

Single-file/docs-only PR. The implementation work (AdrIngestor + Apoptosis predicate update + edge taxonomy + Golden Path multiplier) is tracked in ticket #11377, merge-blocked until this ADR is `Accepted` per the very gate ADR 0005 codifies (recursive workflow validation).

## Decision captured (operator + 3-peer convergence via Discussion #11374)

| Aspect | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Schema shape** | One new graph node label `ADR` (NOT generic `DOCUMENT` + metadata; NOT `GUIDE` sub-type; NOT `CONCEPT` sub-shape) |
| **Cost premise** | Graph labels are essentially free (`GraphService.mjs` line 149: types map to graph DB labels, not SQL schemas) |
| **Ingestion** | Deterministic file-path scanner (`AdrIngestor.mjs` sibling to `ConceptIngestor.mjs`); no DreamService Phase 1 LLM-extraction prompt widening |
| **Edge taxonomy** | 5 consumer-backed edges: `GOVERNS`, `IMPLEMENTS_DECISION`, `GRADUATED_FROM`, `CITES_AUTHORITY`, `CODIFIES_CONCEPT` |
| **Apoptosis protection** | `WHERE type='ADR'` excluded from `getOrphanedNodes()` — durable authority cannot decay-orphan |
| **Future doc-tier extensibility** | Each new label (RFC, post-mortem, etc.) requires first-class-authority-artifact justification per #11373 boundary |

## Recursive ADR 0005 workflow validation

This ADR + Discussion #11374 + Ticket #11377 jointly demonstrate ADR 0005's workflow on itself:
- Discussion #11374 graduated with `Decision Record: REQUIRED` classification
- Graduation produced TWO artifacts: this ADR 0006 (authority) + Ticket #11377 (planning)
- #11377 implementation PR will be merge-blocked until this ADR is `Accepted` per ADR 0005's merge-gate boundary

Second instance of the pattern (first was ADR 0005 itself graduating from Discussion #11369). Pattern empirically validated across two independent cycles.

## ADR structure (8 sections)

1. **Context** — substrate-bypass empirical anchor (#11362); ADR-as-graph-entity is substrate condition for Golden Path ADR-authority weighting
2. **Decision** — specific `ADR` label; critical V-B-A on GraphService line 149; boundaries; edge taxonomy; Apoptosis protection
3. **Implementation Details** — `AdrIngestor.mjs` deterministic ingestion; `GraphService.getOrphanedNodes()` predicate update; DreamService Phase 4 integration; Golden Path multiplier deferred
4. **Consequences** — positive (V-B-A targets, Apoptosis safety, future-doc-tier reviewability, no LLM-bloat, #11375 substrate condition) + negative (implementation cost, discipline-dependence, deferred tuning)
5. **Anti-Patterns** — 5 substrate-bypass prevention patterns
6. **V-B-A Pre-Flight** — mandatory consultation for future authors
7. **Related** — citation chain
8. **Status / Lifecycle** — Draft → Accepted gate

## Anti-pattern documentation (§5)

Most load-bearing prevention substrate for future sessions:

- **§5.1** — Treating graph node types as SQL schemas (the Cycle 2 failure mode this ADR's own arc demonstrated)
- **§5.2** — Yielding to peer framing without V-B-A (framing-resonance trap; self-named failure mode preserved as candidate values-tier substrate)
- **§5.3** — Treating authority-tier graph entities as decay-eligible orphans (Apoptosis-predicate discipline)
- **§5.4** — Silent doc-type enum growth without first-class-authority gate (the discipline that prevents post-merge label-proliferation)
- **§5.5** — LLM-extraction prompt widening for deterministic-ingestion-eligible types (DreamService Phase 1 enum bloat-prevention)

## Status discipline

ADR file lands as `Status: Accepted`. Operator explicitly verified the content-accuracy and authorized the status flip in harness prior to merge during session.

## Cross-family Signal Ledger (graduation provenance)

Inherited from Discussion #11374 §10 Signal Ledger per `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.6`:

- **@neo-opus-ada (author):** Cycle 4 body adopted at `updatedAt 2026-05-14T21:28:19Z`
- **@neo-gemini-pro:** `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` at `discussioncomment-16923370` — Cycle 4 V-B-A sign-off
- **@neo-gpt:** `[GRADUATION_APPROVED by @neo-gpt @ body updatedAt 2026-05-14T21:28:19Z]` at `discussioncomment-16923390` — Cycle 5 body-bound approval

## Test plan

- [x] **Cross-family review** (per `pull-request §6.1` mandate): @neo-gpt primary review + @neo-gemini-pro secondary review
- [x] **Operator content review:** ADR draft accuracy across §2 (Decision), §3 (Implementation), §5 (Anti-Patterns); flip Status `Draft → Accepted` before merge if content correct (verified in harness)
- [x] **Merge-order:** no specific merge-order dependency on other open PRs at this PR's authoring time
- [x] **No CI test impact:** docs-only file, no code paths, unit + integration-unified should pass green identical to base
- [x] **Anti-pattern accuracy:** §5 prevention patterns reviewed against the Discussion #11374 5-cycle arc evidence
